page.title: Changelog
page.subtitle: Release history and patch notes for Terraria Patcher.
page.currentVersionDesc: You are currently running this app version.
page.noEntriesDesc: Add release entries to version.json and translations to changelog.json.

v1_1_11.title: CoreCLR Double Init Fix
v1_1_11.summary: Fixes the edge_coreclr assertion crash on Windows by preventing double CoreCLR initialization in packaged builds.
changes: Removed the `EDGE_NATIVE` environment variable pre-setting before loading `edge.js`, which could cause the native `.node` module to be loaded under two different cache keys, triggering a double `coreclr::bind()` assertion crash.
Edge.js now handles all native module resolution on its own — it already has built-in platform-aware logic that correctly finds the right binary for each platform.
Linux AppImage patching remains unaffected (edge.js finds `build/Release/` on its own).

v1_1_10.title: Prebuilt Edge Native Fix
v1_1_10.summary: Fixes the Windows edge_coreclr assertion crash in release/update builds by using the correct prebuilt native module instead of the CI-compiled one.
changes: Windows packaged builds now use the prebuilt `edge_coreclr.node` from `lib/native/` instead of the CI-compiled `build/Release/` binary, fixing the `g_coreclr == nullptr` assertion crash.
CI release workflow now removes the CI-compiled edge native on Windows to prevent it from being packaged over the correct prebuilt.
Updated `asarUnpack` to include both `build/Release/` and `lib/native/` paths, ensuring Linux (CI-compiled) and Windows (prebuilt) both work.
Made edge native path resolution platform-aware: Windows/macOS use prebuilt from `lib/native/`, Linux uses CI-compiled from `build/Release/`.

v1_1_9.title: Packaged Edge Path Fix
v1_1_9.summary: Fixes Windows patching by loading electron-edge-js from the correct app.asar.unpacked path and tightening the native runtime check.
changes: Updated packaged edge entry/native paths to use app.asar.unpacked/node_modules/electron-edge-js, preventing edge_coreclr assertions in release builds.
Improved error messaging to point to the exact native path expected in packaged Windows builds.

v1_1_8.title: Windows Patch Assert Fix
v1_1_8.summary: Fixes the Windows edge_coreclr assertion when patching from release builds and cleans up packaging validation.
changes: Removed the duplicate packaged copy of electron-edge-js that caused edge_coreclr to load from a wrong path in release builds.
Adjusted release validation to point at the correct unpacked edge path in Windows bundles.
Ensured Windows patching runs with a single edge runtime copy, preventing the assertion failure.

v1_1_7.title: UI Overflow + Release Artifact Guardrails
v1_1_7.summary: Fixes long backup path overflow in the patch modal and hardens release artifact validation for Windows builds.
changes: Fixed patch modal layout overflow when backup paths are very long (paths now wrap correctly instead of breaking the dialog).
Added release pipeline checks to ensure tag/version consistency before packaging.
Added Windows packaged artifact validation to guarantee `patcher-edge-js` runtime files are present before publishing.

v1_1_6.title: Windows Edge Runtime Hardening
v1_1_6.summary: Prevents packaged Windows builds from loading a mismatched edge module/runtime pair after updates.
changes: Packaged builds now force-load `electron-edge-js` from `resources/patcher-edge-js`.
Added defensive checks to ensure the loaded edge module exports `edge.func` before patch initialization.
Removed packaged fallback to bundled node_modules edge path to avoid JS/native mismatch crashes.
Improved error clarity when packaged edge resources are missing.

v1_1_5.title: Runtime Sync Guardrails
v1_1_5.summary: Improves update reliability by preventing stale runtime/plugin files from being reused after upgrades.
changes: Added version-aware runtime sync so managed patch files are refreshed after app updates.
Added startup runtime maintenance for configured installs with plugin support enabled.
Serialized runtime sync operations to prevent concurrent copy/clean races during patch and sync actions.
Added a dedicated runtime repair IPC path for safe forced re-sync flows.

v1_1_4.title: Hotfix: Windows Patching Unblocked
v1_1_4.summary: Restores the Windows patch flow that regressed after 1.1.3 and adds a small stability tweak.
changes: Fixed the Windows patch pipeline (edge/bridge load) so patching works again on Windows installs.
Adjusted runtime detection to avoid false blockers when .NET 10 is already installed.
Minor stability and startup ordering tweaks.

v1_1_3.title: Stability Update (Windows + Linux)
v1_1_3.summary: Follow-up stability update after 1.1.2, focused on safer startup and patch initialization.
changes: Fixed a Windows startup/assert issue that could happen when initializing patch runtime components.
Kept Linux AppImage patching flow stable after the previous edge loading fix.
Improved bridge initialization order to reduce startup conflicts.

v1_1_2.title: Linux AppImage Fix
v1_1_2.summary: Linux-focused fix to make patching work normally on AppImage builds.
changes: Fixed Linux AppImage patch failures where `electron-edge-js` could not find the native module `edge_coreclr.node`.
Adjusted packaging/loading of `electron-edge-js` so it works correctly on Linux.

v1_1_1.title: Runtime Checks & Plugin Stability
v1_1_1.summary: Adds .NET 10/Mono prerequisite checks, stabilizes plugin loading across Windows/Linux, and fixes Save Position persistence.
changes: Added a cross-platform .NET 10 runtime check with a clear prompt when the runtime is missing or outdated.
Added Mono compiler detection on Linux/macOS when plugin support is enabled, with distro-specific install hints.
Improved plugin loader compatibility across Windows and Linux, reducing edge-js startup/assert errors.
Fixed JourneyEverywhere fallback so it no longer alters character difficulty while remaining compatible with Linux builds.
Fixed SavePosition plugin so last position is restored correctly instead of spawning at world borders.
General plugin bug fixes and Linux compatibility tweaks.

v1_1_0.title: Update UX Refresh + Linux Patch Support
v1_1_0.summary: Improved updater status visibility, polished the Config updates panel, added profile reset plus Terraria path auto-detection, and officially enabled Linux patching support.
changes: Officialized Terraria patching support on Linux (FNA builds), including plugin support with automatic FNA/XNA plugin loader selection.
Migrated the app runtime from .NET Framework 4.7.2 to .NET 10.0.0 for better cross-platform support.
Added updater status severity colors (green/yellow/red) based on how many releases behind the installed version is.
Improved Home and Config updater status consistency using the same update-state color logic.
Refined the App Updates panel layout in Config (better spacing and version cards).
Added a collapsible patch notes section in Config for cleaner update details viewing.
Added a Reset App Data button to clear saved profile/config settings and restore defaults.
Added Terraria path auto-detection for common Steam/GOG install locations on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
Improved platform-specific file picker filters when selecting the Terraria executable/game app manually.

v1_0_2.title: Updater & Release Pipeline Fixes
v1_0_2.summary: Fixed updater download issues caused by release asset naming mismatches and improved release pipeline reliability.
changes: Standardized release artifact names (Windows/macOS/Linux) to keep updater metadata and uploaded assets consistent.
Improved updater error handling when release files are not fully published yet (latest.yml/setup/blockmap still processing).
Replaced noisy 404 download errors in the UI with a cleaner translated message.
Updated the GitHub Actions release workflow to build from the selected release tag (including manual runs), preventing version/tag mismatches.

v1_0_1.title: UI & Updater Polish
v1_0_1.summary: Improved update release notes rendering and polished the Home/Changelog interface.
changes: Rendered updater release notes correctly (Markdown + HTML) instead of showing raw tags.
Added safer, styled release notes rendering for dark and light themes.
Improved updater private-repository error message with a clear developer contact link.
Refined Home page layout/cards alignment and reduced visual clutter.
Polished icon consistency and bullet alignment across Home and Changelog.

v1_0_0.title: Initial Public Release
v1_0_0.summary: First public release with functional patching, plugin support and settings persistence.
changes: Implemented Electron -> edge-js -> C# bridge patching with Mono.Cecil.
Added Terraria 1.4.5.x plugin support with runtime resource sync and plugin hooks.
Added Plugins.ini editor with typed fields (hotkey, boolean, number and text).
Added profile import/export for patch and configuration preferences.
Improved packaging to include bridge DLLs, plugin resources and main-process locales.
